meta.setting: Trail junctions, cattle rails, campfires, claim roads, and work camps around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with practical marks, camp language, warnings, reputation, and hard choices
meta.issue_title: Page: Brands, Blazes, and Camp Calls

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional roads, mining communities, work camps, ranching, logging, and settlement context.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California commerce, roads, courthouse records, and old town material.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek Native community context, used here mainly to avoid appropriating or inventing Native sign systems.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Brands, Blazes, and Camp Calls cast/support page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H2 Brands, Blazes, and Camp Calls
square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented mark note, camp warning, or work saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Plain Marks of Road and Stock
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing marks, brands, tree blazes, camp calls, and work sayings used to navigate, warn, claim, and remember
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or uses
12 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable mark, warning, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate this as a marks-and-sayings page with western signs only: brands, blaze cuts, cairns, boot scrapes, knots, chalk, grease pencil, hand signals, and camp calls.
Twelve future entries should be practical table tools, not secret symbols or decorative lore.
Include caution around not turning Native signs into invented code; use settler work marks and fictional county conventions unless the future writer has direct source guidance.
Make every mark useful and a little suspicious.
Do not include final entries, final sayings, or final mark text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: This page should feel carved by pocketknife and smoked by campfire, but never mystical. A mark is a sentence left for somebody who may arrive too late.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A mark scene should ask who left it, who misunderstood it, who changed it, and who reached camp because of it.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western work-mark and camp-call language only.
Do not invent Native signs, sacred systems, fake alphabets, or secret mystical marks.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/brands-blazes-and-camp-calls.jpg.
No finished page prose, final entries, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred-invented, fake-tribal, or old-world alphabet framing
do not make the marks decorative only; each future entry must serve play
do not use modern trail signs, spray paint, vehicles, plastic props, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence and includes 12 future H4 mark/call slots.
The future page is about practical western marks, warnings, camp calls, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
